spec_match.rationale: Uses ::part(trigger)::after with content: attr(data-tip) and chained :hover::after / :focus-visible::after — exactly the kind of new chain the spec relaxation unblocks.

interactive.rationale: Four real cards with hover/focus tooltips, plus a live editor that rewrites data-tip and a mode toggle that swaps to a JS-injected light-DOM workaround.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises ::part(trigger)::after { content: attr(data-tip) } with a pre-130 simulation toggle — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Pre-130 fallback is shown (good) but no probe surfaces what's still rejected; also the attr() inheritance edge case (data-tip lives on the host, attr() resolving against the shadow part) is the genuinely interesting subtlety and is never called out.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5078764563611648 — WRONG ID, same copy-paste mistake as focus-ring-part. Feature index uses 5195333643272192.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, apply-btn overrides background AND color together on hover, tooltip uses --text-black on --bg-ivory which passes AA easily.

openQuestions.title: Fix the chromestatus feature ID in references
openQuestions.detail: Same bug as focus-ring-part. The link reads chromestatus.com/feature/5078764563611648 but the canonical id for this feature is 5195333643272192. The id is the slug-recovery anchor (CLAUDE.md rule #3) so a wrong value here is a real hazard.

openQuestions.title: Surface the attr() resolution subtlety
openQuestions.detail: attr(data-tip) on a ::part()::after resolves against the shadow part's host attributes — that's a genuinely subtle and useful pattern that the demo uses but never explains. Add a one-paragraph callout next to the editor so readers learn the attribute-lookup model.

openQuestions.title: Add ::part()::before example alongside ::part()::after
openQuestions.detail: ::before is also newly chainable and is arguably more useful for icons. Add a single ::before example (e.g. a leading status dot) so the page covers the full ::before/::after pair, not just one side.

openQuestions.title: Probe combinators rejection
openQuestions.detail: Add a small CSS.supports() readout for still-rejected chains so the visitor understands the limit. Combinators after ::part() (e.g. ::part(trigger) > .tip) are the obvious one and would be useful in this exact tooltip scenario, which makes their rejection worth showing.

summary: Strong tooltip demo with a working pre-130 contrast mode. Same wrong-feature-id bug as focus-ring-part — both pages need the chromestatus link corrected before the slug-recovery script runs against them.
